title: SpaceJoinInput struct encodes XOR invariant; callers will construct invalid inputs by accident
description: SpaceJoinInput has two Option fields (invite_code, space_id) with the documented invariant that exactly one must be Some. This is not enforced at the type level — the struct can be constructed in three invalid states (both None, both Some, or a valid state where the author just forgot the Other field). The runtime check in space_join is correct but the error only surfaces when the method is called, not at construction. Because the struct is public and derives Debug but not any From/Into, a future contributor writing SpaceJoinInput { invite_code: None, space_id: None } compiles fine and produces a confusing error at call time. The preferred encoding for this invariant is an enum: SpaceJoinBy::InviteCode(&str) | SpaceJoinBy::SpaceId(&str). This is a public API that callers will depend on. Changing it later is a semver-breaking rename.
